items.title: Front offices originally worked under the assumption that the NBA salary cap would rise the max 10% each season to $228 million in 2029-30.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Front offices originally worked under the assumption that the NBA salary cap would rise the max 10% each season to $228 million in 2029-30.

Now they’re projected to have $35m less than expected that season.

Production per dollar has become one of the most important stats in the league.

images.alt: Line chart titled "The NBA's Vanishing Cap Room," subtitled "Original salary cap projections vs. current projections." Two lines from 2024-25 to 2029-30: original projections (gray dashed) rising from $140M to $228M, and current projections (navy solid) rising from $140M to $193M. Both lines match through 2025-26 at $155M, then diverge — gaps of −$6M in '26-27, −$15M in '27-28, −$25M in '28-29, and −$35M in '29-30, shown as a shaded pink area between the lines. Data: Dunc'd On Prime. Viz: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social.

items.title: "It’s a job within a job — just stay ready.”
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
"It’s a job within a job — just stay ready.”

Kam Jones, Pacers' 2025 second-round pick, signed a two-way contract with the Bucks. He was waived by the Bulls last week.

It's a return to Milwaukee for Jones, 24, who played at Marquette from 2021-25.

www.fieldhousefiles.com/p/1on1-kam-j...

media.title: Kam Jones on Pacers exit: ‘That’s just the business of the NBA’
media.description: One year after the Pacers traded up to draft him, Jones was dealt to Chicago as Indiana again moved into the 38th pick — this time to select Purdue guard Braden Smith.

items.title: I did mention that there was another transaction for the LA Clippers to make...
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
I did mention that there was another transaction for the LA Clippers to make...

Rui Hachimura stays in Southern California

I am told that he is on a two-year deal, and the Clippers have a TEAM option on him in 2027.
